mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE declines below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and core inflation expectations signal accommodative monetary policy and stable real rates, creating tailwinds for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL < 2% in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns when INDPRO rises by > 2% YoY during FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts indicate strong residential demand that drives construction sector growth and corporate revenue visibility, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns despite HOUST increasing by > 10% MoM in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals robust employment conditions that increase consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when PAYEMS increases by > 100k in 40%+ of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales figures indicate strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns despite RSAFS increasing by > 5% MoM in 30%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth indicates expanding economic activity that boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns when GDPC1 grows by > 2% YoY in 40%+ of cases.
